SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.controllerdevice

Class ControllerPerformanceMetrics

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.controllerdevice.ControllerPerformanceMetrics
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public final class ControllerPerformanceMetrics extends Object implements Serializable
Loop-tuning key performance indicators (KPIs) computed from a controller event log or from raw time / process-value / set-point / controller-output arrays.

The helper computes the standard metrics used when comparing PID tunings on a control loop: the integral error criteria (IAE, ISE, ITAE), process-value variability, controller-output (valve) travel and reversals, and the settling time. It lets tuning studies report consistent numbers without re-implementing the definitions in every task.

Instances are immutable and are created through the static factory methods fromEventLog(List) and fromArrays(double[], double[], double[], double[]).

Version:
$Id: $Id
Author:
esol

    Compute loop KPIs from a controller event log.
    int
    getControllerOutputReversals()
    Number of controller-output (valve) direction reversals.
    double
    getControllerOutputTravel()
    Total controller-output (valve) travel, the sum of the absolute output increments.
    double
    getDuration()
    Total time span of the samples.
    double
    getIntegralAbsoluteError()
    Integral of the absolute error (IAE).
    double
    getIntegralSquaredError()
    Integral of the squared error (ISE).
    double
    getIntegralTimeAbsoluteError()
    Integral of time multiplied by the absolute error (ITAE), time referenced to the first sample.
    double
    getMeanProcessValue()
    Mean process value over the samples.
    double
    getPeakAbsoluteError()
    Peak absolute control error observed.
    double
    getProcessValueStandardDeviation()
    Process-value variability, expressed as the population standard deviation of the process value.
    int
    getSampleCount()
    Number of samples used in the calculation.
    double
    getSettlingTime()
    Settling time, the time of the last sample where the error exceeded the settling band, relative to the first sample.
    double
    getSettlingTolerance()
    Settling band tolerance used for the settling-time calculation.
    String
    toString()

    • ControllerPerformanceMetrics

      private ControllerPerformanceMetrics(int sampleCount, double duration, double integralAbsoluteError, double integralSquaredError, double integralTimeAbsoluteError, double meanProcessValue, double processValueStandardDeviation, double peakAbsoluteError, double controllerOutputTravel, int controllerOutputReversals, double settlingTime, double settlingTolerance)

  • Method Details

    • fromEventLog

      public static ControllerPerformanceMetrics fromEventLog(List<ControllerEvent> eventLog)
      Compute loop KPIs from a controller event log using the default settling tolerance.
      Parameters:
      eventLog - list of ControllerEvent entries in chronological order
      Returns:
      computed performance metrics

    • fromEventLog

      public static ControllerPerformanceMetrics fromEventLog(List<ControllerEvent> eventLog, double settlingTolerance)
      Compute loop KPIs from a controller event log.
      Parameters:
      eventLog - list of ControllerEvent entries in chronological order
      settlingTolerance - settling band as a fraction of max(|setpoint|, 1); negative values are clamped to 0
      Returns:
      computed performance metrics

    • fromArrays

      public static ControllerPerformanceMetrics fromArrays(double[] time, double[] processValue, double[] setPoint, double[] controllerOutput)
      Compute loop KPIs from raw arrays using the default settling tolerance.
      Parameters:
      time - simulation time samples [s]
      processValue - measured process value samples
      setPoint - set-point samples
      controllerOutput - controller output (valve) samples
      Returns:
      computed performance metrics

    • fromArrays

      public static ControllerPerformanceMetrics fromArrays(double[] time, double[] processValue, double[] setPoint, double[] controllerOutput, double settlingTolerance)
      Compute loop KPIs from raw arrays.
      Parameters:
      time - simulation time samples [s]
      processValue - measured process value samples
      setPoint - set-point samples
      controllerOutput - controller output (valve) samples
      settlingTolerance - settling band as a fraction of max(|setpoint|, 1); negative values are clamped to 0
      Returns:
      computed performance metrics
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the input arrays are null or of differing length

      public int getSampleCount()
      Number of samples used in the calculation.
      Returns:
      sample count

      Total time span of the samples.
      Returns:
      duration [s]

      public double getIntegralAbsoluteError()
      Integral of the absolute error (IAE).
      Returns:
      IAE in (process-value unit).s

    • getIntegralSquaredError

      public double getIntegralSquaredError()
      Integral of the squared error (ISE).
      Returns:
      ISE in (process-value unit)^2.s

    • getIntegralTimeAbsoluteError

      public double getIntegralTimeAbsoluteError()
      Integral of time multiplied by the absolute error (ITAE), time referenced to the first sample.
      Returns:
      ITAE in (process-value unit).s^2

      public double getMeanProcessValue()
      Mean process value over the samples.
      Returns:
      mean process value

      public double getProcessValueStandardDeviation()
      Process-value variability, expressed as the population standard deviation of the process value.
      Returns:

      Peak absolute control error observed.

      peak absolute error

      Total controller-output (valve) travel, the sum of the absolute output increments.
      Returns:
      total controller-output travel in output units

    • getControllerOutputReversals

      Number of controller-output (valve) direction reversals.
      Returns:
      reversal count

      public double getSettlingTime()
      Settling time, the time of the last sample where the error exceeded the settling band, relative to the first sample.
      Returns:
      settling time [s]

      Settling band tolerance used for the settling-time calculation.
